title: Gli articoli determinativi
grammar_features_introduced: the seven definite article forms: il, lo, l' (m sing); i, gli (m plur); la, l' (f sing); le (f plur)
phonological rule for masculine: il (default) / lo (s+cons, z, gn, ps, pn, x, y) / l' (vowel)
plural transformations: il → i, lo → gli, l' (m) → gli, la → le, l' (f) → le
contrast with French (single plural form) and Spanish (phonological symmetry)
pronunciation_features_introduced: article + noun as a phonological unit (article liaison into noun)
notes: First A1 grammar lesson, English-framed with French comparisons. Article forms in English <em> emphasis (so they’re not themselves wrapped as Italian spans — avoids sub-threshold drills for 2-char articles). All Italian examples are article+noun phrases, which are drill-length and let the learner hear each article in its natural phonological context.
